name: Xiaomi 15 Ultra
description: Xiaomi's ultimate flagship combines a Leica Summilux quad-camera system headlined by a 200 MP 1-inch main sensor, Snapdragon 8 Elite, a stunning 6.73-inch OLED display, and 90W wireless charging — all in a titanium-framed body. The most camera-centric Android flagship available today.

specifications.value: 6.73-inch OLED, 1–120 Hz LTPO, 3200 × 1440 px, 3000 nits peak
200 MP 1" main Leica Summilux (f/1.63) + 50 MP ultrawide (f/1.8) + 50 MP periscope telephoto 3× + 50 MP telephoto 5×
5500 mAh, 90W HyperCharge wired, 90W wireless, 10W reverse wireless
HyperOS 2 (Android 15), 4 years OS + 5 years security updates
5G, Wi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 5.4, NFC, UWB, IR blaster
Micro-curved OLED with Xiaomi Dragon Crystal glass, titanium frame, eco-leather back

additionalInfo.value: Xiaomi Corporation, No. 68 Qinghe Middle Street, Haidian District, Beijing, China
Xiaomi 15 Ultra, 90W HyperCharge adapter, USB-C cable, protective case, SIM tool

reviews.title: The best mobile camera system in existence
reviews.reviewText: I've been a hobbyist photographer for 10 years and the Leica quad-camera system on this phone has me leaving my mirrorless at home. The 1-inch sensor in low light is extraordinary. 90W wireless charging is magic.

reviews.title: Absolutely stunning photographs
reviews.reviewText: The Leica colour science gives photos a film-like quality that no other phone can replicate. The 5× telephoto is tack-sharp even in difficult lighting. This is the phone for anyone who takes photography seriously.

reviews.title: Incredible hardware, HyperOS learning curve
reviews.reviewText: The hardware is objectively the best available in 2026. HyperOS 2 takes a few days to get used to if you're coming from stock Android or iOS, but once you know where everything is it's actually very capable. Don't let the UI put you off.

reviews.title: 90W wireless charging changed my life
reviews.reviewText: I charge this wirelessly in under an hour. The concept of slow wireless charging is dead. Combined with the 5500 mAh battery, I have never once run out of charge on this phone. Plus, the camera is genuinely extraordinary.

reviews.title: Heavy but worth it
reviews.reviewText: At 227 g it's the heaviest phone I've owned. After a few days you barely notice. What you do notice is the insane camera system producing stunning results in every scenario — night mode, portraits, macro, landscapes. A true camera phone.
